text.title: Triage Output Contract
text.summary: The triage protocol produces a single table per session, committed alongside the session notes. The table is the input to backlog grooming and to the same-day-fix ticket creation.

list.item: frequency_band — SINGLE / FEW / MANY / MOST plus raw "k/N".
context_modifier — REVENUE-CRITICAL / REGULATED / INTERNAL or none.
rationale — one line, mandatory for overrides.
created_ticket — issue tracker link if action != DISCARD.
For each SAME-DAY-FIX: a ticket created and linked.
Escalation log entry if any SAME-DAY-FIX action.

antipattern.reason: Violates r1. Future triages can't compare or audit.

antipattern.description: SAME-DAY-FIX action without a created ticket OR without an owner.
antipattern.reason: Same-day means a real human, on the calendar, today. Without it the action is a hope.

antipattern.description: BACKLOG action for a sev-4 finding with no override rationale.
antipattern.reason: Sev-4 + BACKLOG is a serious downgrade requiring written justification.

list.item: Every finding from the session appears in the table.
Action is matrix-derived (or has a written override).
SAME-DAY-FIX actions have tickets and an escalation log entry.
